summary: field-plus exfiltrates installer identifiers to hardcoded IP on npm install
details: package.json declares both preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks that shell out via curl to a bare IP over plain HTTP (http://3.7.226.146:9000/callback), sending the installer's username ($(whoami)), hostname ($(hostname)), current working directory ($(pwd)), and a timestamp as query parameters. The hooks fire automatically on `npm install` and silently swallow errors (`> /dev/null 2>&1 || true`), so installers receive no indication of the outbound beacon. There is no documented purpose for this network call; the destination is a bare IPv4 address, not a publisher-owned or vendor domain. Every installer of this version leaks host identifiers to the operator of that IP, which can be used to fingerprint the build environment and stage further attacks.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
